description: insert_me mutation strategy catalog. Each entry describes one CWE family, its current implementation maturity, Juliet-derived rationale, and planning-layer suitability metadata.

maturity_levels.IMPLEMENTED_AND_CORPUS_ADMITTED: Implemented in patcher.py, has sandbox seeds, quality gate pass rate >= 80%, reproducibility 100%, corpus-admitted. Suitable for planning layer.
maturity_levels.IMPLEMENTED_EXPERIMENTAL: Implemented in patcher.py with unit tests, but not corpus-admitted: insufficient sandbox coverage or quality gate not yet run. Planning layer uses with reduced confidence.
maturity_levels.PARTIAL: Prototype exists but quality gate pass rate < 80%.
maturity_levels.PLANNED: Design documented; multi-line handler infrastructure available; implementation is next-priority.
maturity_levels.CANDIDATE: Feasibility assessed; no design or implementation yet.
maturity_levels.DISABLED: Was implemented; disabled due to quality issues.

strategies.description: Transforms malloc(<expr>) to malloc((<expr>) - 1). Introduces a one-byte undercount in a heap allocation. When the caller writes the expected number of bytes, the write overflows the allocated region.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE122 patterns: allocate a buffer, write exactly N bytes. Off-by-one in allocation size is the canonical buffer-overflow insertion.
strategies.source_pattern: malloc(<arithmetic-expr>) -- prefer lines with n * sizeof(T) or similar arithmetic
strategies.sink_pattern: memcpy / strcpy / field-write using the allocated pointer
common_failure_modes: malloc(sizeof(T)) without arithmetic -- mutation is syntactically valid but semantically trivial
malloc result unused -- NOOP audit if pointer not written through
strategies.notes: Most reliable strategy. Scores 0.75 in quality gate (arithmetic-expression malloc lines).

strategies.description: Inserts free(<ptr>); immediately before a pointer dereference (ptr->field or *ptr). The freed pointer is then used on the very next line, producing a use-after-free.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE416 patterns: allocate, use, then a free followed immediately by another use of the same pointer.
strategies.source_pattern: ptr->field or *ptr dereference -- prefer lines where ptr was malloc'd within the same function scope
strategies.sink_pattern: Any write or read through the freed pointer on the immediately following line
common_failure_modes: Loop-body dereference: free inside a loop causes repeated free on second iteration (secondary flaw)
Sub-malloc: free before malloc(ptr->field) creates a double-use issue
strategies.notes: Reliable strategy with multi-signal scoring (prior malloc in scope, loop-body and conditional-guard penalties, sub-malloc penalty).

strategies.description: Inserts a duplicate free(<ptr>); immediately before an existing free() call. The heap allocator receives two calls to free the same pointer, which is undefined behaviour and exploitable on most platforms.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE415 patterns: allocate, free, free again. Duplicate free is the canonical double-free pattern.
strategies.source_pattern: free(<simple-ptr>) or free(ptr->field) -- prefer non-loop, non-guard-expression locations
strategies.sink_pattern: The existing free() call that the duplicate is inserted before
common_failure_modes: free() inside a loop: inserting a duplicate causes the loop-body double-free on every iteration
free() in conditional guard: inserting before error-handling free may create unreachable double-free
strategies.notes: Works on any simple free(ptr) or free(ptr->field). Quality gate ~90%.

strategies.cwe_name: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
strategies.description: Replaces a free(<ptr>); call with a comment /* CWE-401: free(ptr) removed - memory leak */. The heap-allocated object is never released, causing a memory leak.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE401 patterns: allocate memory, forget to free it. Removing the free() call is the minimal-mutation equivalent.
strategies.source_pattern: free(<simple-ptr>) -- same as insert_double_free but the mutation removes rather than duplicates
strategies.sink_pattern: End of function scope or error-return path where the allocation is no longer reachable
common_failure_modes: Detector may only report low-severity leak warning -- semantic matching needed for evaluation

strategies.description: Replaces a null-check guard (if (!ptr) return;) with a comment, leaving a NULL dereference reachable at the subsequent ptr->field line. Uses the multi-line handler API.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE476 patterns: null pointer dereference after a guard that protects a subsequent pointer use.
strategies.source_pattern: if (!ptr) return; or if (ptr == NULL) return; immediately before a ptr->field dereference
strategies.sink_pattern: ptr->field or *ptr on the line following the removed guard
strategies.corpus_admission_blocker: Handler only matches single-line inline guards. Sandbox files predominantly use multi-line guard style. Only 1 viable target found across 9 sandbox files.
common_failure_modes: Multi-line guard style (if (!ptr) followed by return NULL; on next line) -- handler misses these
Guard followed by abort() rather than return -- no deref after the guard
strategies.notes: Implemented and unit-tested. NOT corpus-admitted. Requires handler enhancement for multi-line guard bodies.

strategies.description: Remove the overflow check (e.g., if (n > MAX) return;) that precedes a multiplication-based malloc(n * sizeof(T)). The resulting allocation is undersized when n overflows.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE190 patterns: integer arithmetic overflows feeding a size calculation.
strategies.source_pattern: malloc(n * sizeof(T)) with a preceding guard like if (n > MAX) return;
strategies.sink_pattern: The malloc call itself, when the guard is removed and n can wrap
strategies.notes: Multi-line blocker resolved (handler infrastructure in place). Implementation follows remove_null_guard pattern.

strategies.description: Reduce the size of a heap allocation feeding a memcpy/strcpy so the write exceeds the allocated region.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE787 patterns: write to a heap buffer whose size was calculated incorrectly.
strategies.source_pattern: malloc(<size-expr>) where size-expr matches the destination argument of a subsequent memcpy/strcpy
strategies.sink_pattern: memcpy/strcpy call writing more bytes than the (now undersized) allocation
strategies.notes: Blocker: requires detecting the allocation that corresponds to the destination pointer.

strategies.description: Mutate an array subscript expression so the index can exceed the buffer bounds, or remove an index bounds check.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Simple single-line feasible; low priority since detectors flag this less consistently than heap issues.
strategies.source_pattern: arr[expr] subscript access in a context where a bounds check exists
strategies.sink_pattern: The read of arr[expr] after the guard is removed
strategies.notes: Simple single-line approach feasible; low priority.

strategies.description: Replace a safe printf("%s", user_str) with printf(user_str), passing the user-controlled string directly as the format argument.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE134 patterns: format string controlled by external input.
strategies.source_pattern: printf/fprintf calls with a string literal format argument followed by a user-controlled string variable
strategies.sink_pattern: The printf call itself after the literal format is removed
strategies.notes: string_operation pattern exists; needs format-string detection to distinguish format arg from data arg.

strategies.description: Mutate a stack buffer size declaration or remove a size check before a strcpy/memcpy so the write overflows the stack buffer.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE121 patterns: stack buffer with a write that exceeds declared size.
strategies.source_pattern: char buf[N]; followed by strcpy(buf, src) without length check
strategies.sink_pattern: The strcpy/memcpy call after the buffer size reduction or guard removal
strategies.notes: Buffer size detection requires declaration scan.

strategies.description: Remove a zero-check guard before a division operation so the divisor can be zero.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Simple guard removal; low security evaluation value since most tools flag divide-by-zero trivially.
strategies.source_pattern: if (divisor == 0) return; or similar guard before a / divisor expression
strategies.sink_pattern: The division expression after the zero guard is removed
strategies.notes: Zero-guard removal; low security evaluation value.

strategies.description: Combine integer overflow with buffer overflow: mutate both the overflow guard removal and the resulting undersized allocation.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Two-stage mutation following Juliet CWE680 chain patterns.
strategies.source_pattern: malloc(n * sizeof(T)) with a preceding integer overflow guard
strategies.sink_pattern: Write through the allocation that is undersized due to the integer overflow
strategies.notes: Two-stage mutation needed; deferred until CWE-190 single-stage is implemented.

strategies.description: Replace sizeof(T) in a malloc call with sizeof(T*), causing the allocation to be pointer-sized instead of struct-sized.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE131 patterns: allocation size calculated incorrectly using wrong sizeof operand.
strategies.sink_pattern: Field write through the undersized allocation
strategies.notes: Syntactically simple single-line mutation. Requires identifying struct type T.

strategies.description: Remove a null-check after malloc or other fallible function calls, leaving the return value unchecked.
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE252 patterns: system call return value ignored.
strategies.source_pattern: ptr = malloc(...); if (!ptr) { ... return; } -- the null-check block is removed
strategies.sink_pattern: The first use of ptr after the (now removed) null check
strategies.notes: Overlaps partially with CWE-476. Distinction: targets error-handling paths after any fallible call.

strategies.cwe_name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input
strategies.description: Replace a size-bounded string copy (strncpy, strncat) with the unbounded equivalent (strcpy, strcat).
strategies.juliet_rationale: Mirrors Juliet CWE120 patterns: unbounded string copy. Replacing strncpy with strcpy is a minimal, syntactically trivial mutation.
strategies.source_pattern: strncpy(dst, src, n) or strncat(dst, src, n) calls
strategies.sink_pattern: The unbounded copy that may overflow dst
strategies.notes: Syntactically simple. Requires strncpy->strcpy substitution (argument count changes from 3 to 2).
